description: The AudioMetadata component provides information about an audio clip. It is automatically added to audio clips recorded with the Microphone Tool and stores data about when, where, and by whom the audio was recorded.

fields.LocationName: Name of the session the audio was recorded in
fields.LocationURL: the URI of the world the audio was recorded in
fields.LocationHost: Host of the session the audio was recorded in
fields.LocationAccessLevel: Access level of the session the audio was recorded in
fields.LocationHiddenFromListing: Whether the session was marked as hidden when the audio was recorded.
fields.TimeTaken: Exact timestamp that the audio was recorded at
fields.TakenBy: The user who recorded the audio
fields.TakenGlobalPosition: the position in global space the microphone tip was at the time of recording.
fields.TakenGlobalRotation: the rotation in global space the microphone tip was at the time of recording.
fields.TakenGlobalScale: the scale in global space the microphone tip was at the time of recording.
fields.AppVersion: Resonite version number of the recording user
fields.UserInfos: Users present at the time of recording
fields.__legacyPresentUsers: legacy list of users present at the time of recording.

usage: This component is essentially a bunch of fields with semantic meaning to them. Usage of this component outside of the microphone tool is straightforward--one simply needs to write to the relevant fields the relevant data. It is common convention to place the component on the same slot that the audio clip resides.
